text.title: Characterization Test Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that turn characterization suites into expensive theater. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Snapshots accepted blindly because "the code does that already."
failure-mode.detector: baseline-signoff is absent OR signoff committed within minutes of snapshot creation with no review markers (no comments, no edits)
failure-mode.repair: Wipe baseline. Re-generate snapshots and walk through each line with a human reviewer. Document the review in baseline-signoff.md (which fixtures were unexpected, which were confirmed).

failure-mode.description: Known bug pinned as expected behavior. Refactor cannot fix it without snapshot diff.
failure-mode.detector: Issue tracker contains an open bug whose reproduction matches a baseline snapshot's content
failure-mode.repair: Re-classify the affected snapshot as "documented bug, intentional refactor-fix" and link the bug ID. The refactor's expected diff is captured separately from accidental diffs.

failure-mode.description: Non-determinism not handled — suite is flaky and devs add @flaky decorators instead of normalizing.
failure-mode.detector: Flaky-decorator or `pytest.mark.skip_if_random` in characterization tests OR tests rerun on CI
failure-mode.repair: Add the missing normalization rule to the central normalize module. No flaky marks in characterization tests — flaky = bug in the suite.

failure-mode.description: PII or production secrets in fixtures committed to git.
failure-mode.detector: grep fixtures/ for @-signs, common name lists, token prefixes, SSN patterns; or use a secret scanner.
failure-mode.repair: Rewrite fixtures with redacted equivalents. Use git filter-repo to scrub history if PII shipped. Update fixture-manifest with redaction confirmation.

failure-mode.description: Sweep stopped at low branch coverage but declared "comprehensive."
failure-mode.detector: coverage-report.json shows branch coverage < 70% on target module AND baseline-signoff is signed
failure-mode.repair: Either lower the claim (this is a partial characterization, refactor only the covered branches) OR add fixtures for uncovered branches before signoff.

failure-mode.description: CI auto-updates snapshots, hiding real regressions in green builds.
failure-mode.detector: CI config contains `--update-snapshots` / `--ci=update` / `-u` in characterization test command
failure-mode.repair: Remove the update flag from CI. Update is a developer action with an explicit commit. CI must fail on snapshot diff.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: When AI generates fixtures from logs, it must not paraphrase or "clean up" the inputs. The fixture must reflect real production noise (extra whitespace, capitalization quirks, optional fields present/absent). Sanitized inputs miss the bugs they would have surfaced.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Baseline signoff is a human gate — one person, named in baseline-signoff.md, claims to have reviewed the snapshots. After signoff, every snapshot diff requires an explicit classification commit. The suite is only as good as the signoff.
